# pfsense_firewall_nat_port_forward (Resource)

[Firewall NAT Port Forward](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/nat/port-forwards.html). Port forward rules redirect traffic from a given external port or range to a specified internal IP and port.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Forward HTTP from WAN to internal web server
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_port_forward" "web_server" {
  interface           = "wan"
  ipprotocol          = "inet"
  protocol            = "tcp"
  source_address      = "any"
  destination_address = "wanip"
  destination_port    = "8080"
  target              = "10.0.1.50"
  local_port          = "80"
  description         = "Forward HTTP to web server"
  associated_rule_id  = "pass"
}

# Forward HTTPS with NAT reflection enabled
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_port_forward" "https_server" {
  interface           = "wan"
  ipprotocol          = "inet"
  protocol            = "tcp"
  destination_address = "wanip"
  destination_port    = "443"
  target              = "10.0.1.50"
  local_port          = "443"
  description         = "Forward HTTPS to web server"
  nat_reflection      = "purenat"
  associated_rule_id  = "pass"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) Description used as the unique identifier for this NAT port forward rule.
- `destination_address` (String) Destination address. Can be 'any', a single IP, a CIDR network, an alias, or a special pfSense interface address (e.g. 'wanip').
- `interface` (String) Network interface this NAT rule applies to (e.g. 'wan', 'lan', 'opt1').
- `ipprotocol` (String) IP address family. Options: `inet`, `inet6`, `inet46`.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol. Options: `tcp`, `udp`, `tcp/udp`, `icmp`, `esp`, `ah`, `gre`, `ipv6`, `igmp`, `pim`, `ospf`.
- `target` (String) Redirect target IP address. The internal IP address to forward traffic to.

### Optional

- `associated_rule_id` (String) Associated filter rule behavior. 'pass' creates an associated firewall rule automatically. Empty string means no associated rule (manual rule creation required).
- `destination_not` (Boolean) Invert the destination address match.
- `destination_port` (String) Destination port or port range. The external port that triggers this NAT rule.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this NAT port forward rule.
- `local_port` (String) Redirect target port. The internal port to forward traffic to. If empty, the destination port is used.
- `nat_reflection` (String) NAT reflection mode. Options: `enable`, `disable`, `purenat`. Empty string means system default.
- `no_rdr` (Boolean) Disable redirection (NOT mode). When enabled, the rule acts as a negation — matching traffic is NOT redirected.
- `source_address` (String) Source address. Can be 'any', a single IP, a CIDR network, an alias, or a special pfSense interface address. Defaults to 'any'.
- `source_not` (Boolean) Invert the source address match.
- `source_port` (String) Source port or port range (e.g. '80' or '8000-9000'). Only applicable for TCP, UDP, or TCP/UDP protocols.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Import a NAT port forward rule by its description
terraform import pfsense_firewall_nat_port_forward.example "Forward HTTP to web server"
```
